title: CropGraph Succession Planting Chains
description:
The third edge layer of the CropGraph, time-sequenced planting chains that turn a single bed into a continuous-harvest schedule. Each chain has a primary crop, a category, and an ordered list of phases. Every phase references a crop slug from crop-calendar.json; the loader cross-references at parse time and fails loud if a slug is missing.

Frost anchor: all phase dates are days relative to the typical last spring frost (LSF) for the gardener's zone. Negative days fall before LSF (early-spring sowings, fall-sown cover crops planted the prior autumn). Day 0 is LSF. Positive days run forward through spring, summer, and fall. The single-anchor model is intentional: chains describe how a sequence flows from spring through fall in one growing-year cadence, not how a single crop maps to two separate anchors.

Climate notes per phase flag the climate types where the base window or sowing cadence materially shifts. Absence of a climate-note key means the base values are appropriate. Notes are gardener-facing prose, not parsed for machine logic.

Evidence grounding: Johnny's Selected Seeds succession planting publications, Cornell Cooperative Extension small-farm planting calendars, UF/IFAS Extension vegetable production handbooks, UC ANR Master Gardener succession publications, SARE Cover Crops field guide, Eliot Coleman's The New Organic Grower succession tables, USDA-NRCS cover crop publications, Rodale Institute farming systems trial. Each chain carries its own citation; the file-level source documents the overall provenance.

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes only; additive optional fields keep the version constant.

source.description: Provenance / citation block for the whole fixture. Per-entry `source` is the authoritative citation; file-level source documents the overall curation pass.

license.description: License under which the data is redistributed (e.g. CC-BY-4.0).

lastUpdated.description: ISO date of the last hand-curation pass.

entries.description: Succession chains. Order is not significant; helpers index by slug at load time.

slug.description: Kebab-case identifier for the chain. Distinct from `primaryCrop` because one crop can appear in multiple chains (e.g. `radish-succession` for fresh use vs. an oilseed-radish cover-crop relay).

primaryCrop.description: The crop the gardener is asking about. Must be a slug from crop-calendar.json. Chain lookups by crop find the chain whose primaryCrop matches, or whose chain.crop entries include the requested slug.

category.description: High-level grouping for filtering and presentation.

chains.description: Ordered phases of the succession. The `phase` field is the canonical order; array order is expected to match.

source.description: Citation for the chain. Multiple sources separated by semicolons.

phase.description: Sequence number within the chain. Phase 1 is the earliest in the year.

crop.description: Crop slug from crop-calendar.json. The loader validates existence at parse time.

sowMethod.description: How this phase enters the bed. `start_indoors` is rare and indicates the gardener starts seeds indoors with no transplant in scope for the chain.

intervalWeeks.description: Cadence within the phase window: a sowing happens every N weeks until the window closes. `null` means a single sowing or transplant event.

startRelativeToFrost.description: Days from last-spring-frost (LSF) when the phase window opens. Negative is before LSF; -210 covers the prior September for overwintering cover crops.

endRelativeToFrost.description: Days from LSF when the phase window closes. Must be >= startRelativeToFrost.

climateNotes.description: Per-climate-type prose notes. Keys are climate types from `climate-types.ts`. Absence means the base window applies.

notes.description: Gardener-facing prose for this phase. Optional.
